bio: Matia sees the world in his own unique way. For him, vision is a gift finding beauty in everything brings him deep satisfaction. He was one of the first in Milan’s creative scene to explore teaching beauty to AI. But soon, he realized his mission was different. No spoilers—he’s still working on it. Now he collaborates with artists as an art director. Everyone has their own truth. Show me yours.
creativeSkills:
Art Direction
Visual Software
Concrete insTall

gallery1Description: Sometime ago I decided to go back in Time and interpolate some frames into Touchdesigner, using a PS2 simulator on a PS Vita.
gallery2Description: This sequence was created after a quick visit at an Ettore Sottsass exposition at Museo della Triennale. He designed some glyphs that recalled those present in the chinese langage and I took the liberty to reinterpret them into a 14 piece collection of generative wallpapers.
bio:
Visual Artist, Sound Designer and Art Director. Perpetually inspired by the creativity of the modern mind. My aesthetic is a result of a multitude of digital skills learned during the years.
All of the projects made as an artist were made with a mixture of several programs, such as TouchDesigner, Blender and the Adobe.

An outstanding master of the sewing arts and an impecable figure in the art of the bespoke.
Luca is a powerhouse of creativity and an inspiration to many young minds that are currently exploring their talents in fashion schools all around Italy.

gallery1Description: Some of the music I listen to, stylized by moi
bio: Since my graduation, I've been working for an innovative startup, focusing on web design, illustration and social media content. I've also expanded my network in the music scene, developing identities and animated posters for various underground clubbing realities from Milan, Rome and Padova.

Chakra, real name Luca Ferraresi (1996), is an Italian producer, songwriter, and singer based in Milan. His music blends introspective lyrics with melodic influences rooted in American RnB. Fully independent and self-sufficient, he has exceeded 35 million streams, collaborated with notable artists like Madman, Nicola Siciliano, and Kina, and become a key figure among Italian producers through pioneering daily Twitch streams engaging over 50,000 followers.

gallery1Description: "Another Vision Of Reality" (AVOR) is the name of my style
bio: Federico Scibilia (Zzzibo, born 1997) is an Italian freelance illustrator and graphic designer known for his dreamy, magical style infused with irony. Collaborating with brands like Nike, Converse, Timberland, Louis Vuitton Foundation, and artists including Maluma and David Lachapelle, Federico specializes in creative direction, constantly exploring new artistic challenges and opportunities.

gallery1Description: First ever Spacewear shooting session, with a powerful concept movie
bio:
Born in Genoa, italy in 2001. At 16 i discovered my passion for fashion and since then i've been doing what i love every single day.
Founder and creative director of TMMT, a fashion start up created in 2019 which aims to spread innovation in the fashion industry by creating clothes with new sewing techniques and long workmanships. We are trying to create our style and a new vision based on future and innovation.

Enrico began studying photography at art high school, continuing with courses in photography and directing at USC Los Angeles (2015). He graduated from NABA Milan in Media Design, including an Erasmus in Santa Fe. Specializing in musical photography and reportage, Enrico’s naturalistic, analog style emphasizes authenticity, particularly through Polaroids and film.

JUCK is a musician known for his energetic style and bold persona. With over 3,000 followers on Instagram, JUCK shares music releases and creative content, connecting with fans through a unique, edgy presence and the tagline “J**K me I’m famous.
T H I S B I O W A S N O T M A D E B Y C H A T G P T

Hello, I'm Giacomo "James" Sforza, also known as Twopixx, a graphic designer from Conversano (BA). My project "Colorblaze: a digital dada," born in 2020, aims to use vibrant palettes, images, and humor to break aesthetic rules and invite viewers to reflect on the hidden meaning behind the images. In contrast to meme culture, I seek to elevate humor by enriching it with details and aesthetics, providing a deeper and more engaging experience.

gallery1Description: A collection of som of the work I'm most proud of.
bio: Born in 1995, Wallie is a Bologna-based comic artist renowned for his ironic yet deeply empathetic storytelling. After debuting with  "Solo un altro giorno (2018)", he published "Croce sul cuore" and "Un sogno chiamato Giffoni" (both Feltrinelli, 2020). His direct, sincere style captures authentic emotion with childlike honesty.

EMMA, aka Alessandro Muscogiuri, is a human being born in Germany and raised in Ruvo di Puglia. With diverse influences, EMMA makes music from the heart. His debut project, ERA, established him in the Italian underground scene, followed by ERA LA FINE in December 2023.
His music is haunted by ghosts chasing each other over anxious beats, suffocating shadows stretching endlessly, yet at its core, a deep sense of humanity fights to be heard.

Margherita Caspani was born in Milan on a mid-August evening in 1999.
Not long after, she began drawing—mostly ducks in flight.
Today, Margherita works as a freelance illustrator. After graduating with honors in Illustration and Animation from IED Milan, her illustrations have received international awards in competitions in Europe, the USA, and China.
Every now and then, she still draws ducks in flight.
Her projects mainly include editorial collaborations and work with brands.

gallery1Description: A collection of some of the custom accessories Dav creates on a daily basis
bio: DAV is an italian customizer and designer of sneakers class born in 2000 raised in Barletta. Passionate about art since childhood, as a teenager he discovered the world of sneakers. The combination of the two led him to experiment and create wearable works of art. Starting from 2018 he began his journey as a handmade customizer. His creations, with time and experience, have been noticed, appreciated and commissioned by the collaborations with international artists such as Capo Plaza, Sfera Ebbasta and many others.

After graduating (and having his computer stolen), he discovered "Doodle Art," a creative practice using permanent ink without fear of mistakes or pre-planning. Combining this approach with his studies in Advertising Graphics and Art Direction at NABA Milan, he developed a versatile, distinctive style as a professional illustrator across multiple mediums.

gallery1Title: Stills of "Doc - The Nave, The Church of Creativity"
gallery1Description:
In August 2020, after six months of worldwide lockdown, an artist collective known as The Nave came together in Milan for an artistic residency.
The documentary tells the story of this unique gathering, exploring the power of creativity as a response to hardship and as a tool for human connection.
gallery2Description: A ten-day glimpse into the lives of the artists in the international community of Bussana Vecchia — a former ghost town near Sanremo, now reborn through art.
bio:
filmbysararos (1995) is a Milan-based photographer and videographer. Her visual research has focused from the very beginning on feminine narratives and artistic processes, explored through an intimate, poetic gaze.
Drawn to film and photography from a young age, she crafts visual stories that balance personal intuition with cinematic structure. Whether shooting on 35mm or behind the camera on set, she moves fluidly between commercial projects and independent work.

Jonathan Bocca (1998) is a Tuscanian sculptural designer from Lucca. 
Graduated in Interior and Furniture Design at IED in Florence, he got the chance to experience Fabrica Research Center in Treviso. 
Here he studied and worked on new materials for his sculptures and his objects. 
Peculiarity focuses his works on the use of recycled paper, a material produced by the waste of the main industries of his hometown Lucca.

gallery2Title: Art Direction for ANNA's "Vera Baddie" album cover
bio: Nic Paranoia, real name Nicolo Amoretti, is an art director and visual artist based between Milan and Parma. Known for his innovative graphic work and striking visual style, he collaborates on music, editorial, and cultural projects, bringing a bold, contemporary edge to Italian art and design.

Born in 1998 amid the boom of the Information Superhighway, 19 is a narrative designer currently based in London. With a background in fashion design from New York and experience in fashion PR in Shanghai.
Driven by a deep love for philosophy and cultural studies, her work focuses on building bridges of communication and crafting narratives. 19 believes that we need those voices to connect us together again, maybe mentally, or maybe ideologically. It’s legit enough to make narratives happen in the tenth art —— game.

gallery1Description: Illustration on mixed media ft. Capitano Erri. A re-draw of a new eden for woman empowerment.
gallery2Description: Graphic, font creation, brand identity, garment production, art direction and production for the campaign and shooting.
bio: After graduating in Industrial and Multimedia Design at IUAV Venice, I started moving between fashion, music, and visual communication, always guided by one thing: the search for beauty that makes sense. I’ve never cared much about what I was designing - whether it was a flyer, a collection, or a logo - as long as it felt aligned with a visual story. I wake up thinking about how I want to look and dress that day not out of vanity, but because everything starts with an image. That same instinct shapes how I approach every creative process.
creativeSkills:
Illustration
Branding
Adobe Illustrator
Mixed Media

bio: SoftwareBoy, born in Rome in 2001, is a 23-year-old 3D artist and designer. Growing up passionate about computers and video games, he discovered 3D software during the Covid-19 lockdown, quickly building a following on Instagram and working with over 100 brands, including Adidas and Footlocker. He creates both digital and physical art, like 3D-printed pieces and NFTs. His project SushiSwag pioneered digital/physical streetwear NFTs. Mentioned in Forbes and Highsnobiety, he has spoken publicly about digital art’s future.
